ref: They said to the soldiers, “Tell everyone that Jesus' disciples came during the night and stole his body while you were asleep.
text: na baa yako nyi nelim ba ke doo na, te Yesu kaseela baŋ kɔŋawɔ kaa ŋmɛɛle ɔ woo ŋon.
translated: You are not of this world, but Jesus chose you and called you to be his own.

ref: Jesus left and went into one of their synagogues,
text: Yesu ka lee botɔɔ nɛ, ɔ gyuuwɔ Gyudatena ɔsom deni man.
translated: Jesus said to them, "Truly, truly, I say to you, before Abraham was, I am."

ref: The ones who were wise took along extra oil for their lamps.
text: Mɔna siakaralatena baŋ dɔɔ, baŋ moo nuŋ do porontiase man tela kpu na ŋan doo be kanease man nɛ.
translated: The one who is patient with others, even if they are wrong, will later receive blessings from the one who was once angry with them.

ref: When people mistreat you in one town, hurry to another one. I promise you before you have gone to all the towns of Israel, the Son of Man will come.
text: Nyi akpaa be naase ŋon diyem donɔɔ daale man na, ɛ yeu gyu donɔɔ wɔle man. Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyiaa, ɛ be gyae ɛ ke yɛɛ tom Iserae donɔɔse ŋan man pou taŋ, pɛte maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon maa bese kɔŋ ya.
translated: If a person has something against you, leave your gift there before the altar and go; first be reconciled with that person, then come and offer your gift to the God of Israel.

ref: Don't you think you should show pity to someone else, as I did to you?”
text: Woŋ dɔɔ te nyaŋ mɔ n te wii n dɔɔ ŋon waraŋase, ŋgba mena keŋ maŋ be wii neŋ nɛ ya?’
translated: If you cannot do that which you did before, why do you still call yourself by that name?

ref: “Lord, my servant is at home in such terrible pain that he can't even move.”
text: “Me Gbeŋgyoo lɛɛ maŋ, nawolo nyi me tobaale te tekerii ɔ doo deni man ɔ ne naa diyem nideli.”
translated: When Gbeŋgyoo came, the man said to him, “Sit down and tell me what you have come to do here.”

ref: So when you go to a town or a village, find someone able and willing to have you as their guest and stay with them until you leave.
text: Donɔɔ yaa akuraa kamasɛ keŋ man ɛ gyoo nɛ, ɛ taŋgbɛɛ bɔɔse walaŋ ŋon ɔ kaboena te waa lɛɛ ŋon nɛ dɔɔ, na ɛ ke kyaa ɔ gyaŋ kaboena e ka mo lee donɔɔ kenaŋ man.
translated: When a person is walking alone in the forest and hears a sound behind them, they should not turn around to look, because that sound might be a spirit trying to lead them astray.

ref: So please order the tomb to be carefully guarded for three days. If you don't, his disciples may come and steal his body. They will tell the people he has been raised to life, and this last lie will be worse than the first one.”
text: Mena dɔɔ yela be kaa deke yebɔɔ keŋ na baa gyee ke nideli kelii weeya atoorote keŋ. Na mena ya na ɔ kaseela baŋ baa yo gyu ke ŋmɛɛle e lese na baa yako nyi, ɔ foŋae lee yeŋ man. Na mena ya na, dekara alate kei gyae ka kela gyaŋgba te keŋ.”
translated: What I am saying is that if someone does something wrong to you, you should not take revenge. And if you see that your enemy is in trouble and needs your help, you should help him. And what I am saying is, that is the way to live in peace.

ref: If your eye causes you to sin, poke it out and get rid of it. You would be better off to go into life with only one eye than to have two eyes and be thrown into the fires of hell. The Lost Sheep (Luke
text: Te akpaa nyi n sibii ne yele la te n ne yɛɛ dukum na kulu ke leki. Dei nyi nya mo sibii dokoloŋ gyoo Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ man, kela nyi baa mo neŋ na n sia ala ka do boalaŋ keŋ ke bɛɛ yem ya nɛ man. Nam ŋon ɔ ba yoo nɛ dudu Luka
translated: The elders say that a child who does not listen to advice will one day face hardship. This is why we urge every child to follow the teachings of Wurubuarɛ, so that they may grow up to live a good and peaceful life. I am the one called Dudu Luka.

ref: Jesus said to him, “You may return home now, but don't go into the village.” Who Is Jesus? (Matthew
text: Botɔɔ te Yesu lɔɔ balee ŋon gbɛɛ te ɔ faree e nyiaa, “Na bese gyu donɔɔ keŋ man bela ya. Ta gyu n dɛɛ.” Pita kpa Yesu yɛna nyeelɛɛre ŋon Mateo
translated: Jesus answered him, “Those who love me will obey my teaching. My Father will love them, and we will come to them and live with them.” Peter turned to Jesus and said, “Lord, it is good for us to be here.”

ref: The blind are now able to see, and the lame can walk. People with leprosy are being healed, and the deaf can hear. The dead are raised to life, and the poor are hearing the good news.
text: Siayɛlɛɛsatena ne naa, ɔtakasese ne tɛɛ, baŋ bɛɛ wee kawee yayam nɛ kaweese te taŋ, te deŋela atɔɔsatena ne nyii. Woya ne foŋii, te bɛɛ yako Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan mɔ fa ayematena.
translated: The sky and the earth, the mountains and the rivers, all things that exist and all that will come, were created by the one great spirit. We live by his will, and one day we shall return to Wurubuarɛ’s house where our souls will rest.

ref: But Jesus replied: You want a sign because you are evil and won't believe! But the only sign you will get is the sign of the prophet Jonah.
text: Keŋte Yesu tiranɔɔ fa wɔ nyiaa, “Ɛmɛɛ gyɛŋ wee kei balaŋ, ɛ yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛyɛɛrawɔ te ɛ be yee Wurubuarɛ ya. Ɛ ne gyae nyi maa yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombia na ɛ kena, mɔna mɔɔ gyae maa yɛɛ gyakoloŋ nombii kamasɛ kpu na keŋ be kɔŋ Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Gyona dɔɔ nɛ ya.
translated: Then Jesus said to them, “Truly I tell you, unless you change and become like little children, you will never enter the kingdom of heaven. Therefore, whoever humbles themselves like this little child is the greatest in the kingdom of heaven.

ref: Jesus' mother and brothers came and stood outside. Then they sent someone with a message for him to come out to them.
text: Keŋte Yesu ɔ naa na ɔ naabia kɔŋawɔ kaa seŋ debɔɔman kpila nyi baa baake e fa wɔ.
translated: Jesus said that he himself is the way and the truth and the life, and no one comes to the Father except through him.

ref: Don't you know what the Scriptures mean when they say, “Instead of offering sacrifices to me, I want you to be merciful to others?” If you knew what this means, you would not condemn these innocent disciples of mine.
text: Wurubuarɛ yakowɔ ɔ nombia aŋmaraseŋ see man nyiaa, ‘Mɛɛ gyae bo nyi yaa wii balaŋ waraŋase, na kedeesa te mɛɛ gyae ya. Nyi yeŋ nyii nombia kɛŋa man nɛ, nafɔ ɛ be gyae ɛ ke bu balaŋ baŋ be nombia dei nɛ fɔɔ ya.’
translated: The hunter said to his child, “If I stop hunting and stay at home, hunger will kill me. But if I keep hunting, one day an animal will surely kill me.”

ref: While Joseph was thinking about this, an angel from the Lord appeared to him in a dream. The angel said, “Joseph, the baby that Mary will have is from the Holy Spirit. Go ahead and marry her.
text: Debaŋ keŋ Gyosɛfo na mo mena agyueŋ kei nɛ, te de Gbeŋgyoo Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋolo kaa lese ɔ wose wola e dosɛɛ man, te ɔ yako e nyiaa, “Defidi ɔ naanabii Gyosɛfo, na yee Mɛɛre ke yala ya. Nawolo nyi Wurubuarɛ feliŋ yele la te ɔ kyaŋa fulu kei.
translated: When Joseph's brothers saw that their father was dead, they said, "What if Joseph holds a grudge against us and pays us back for all the wrongs we did to him?" So they sent word to Joseph, saying, "Your father left these instructions before he died."

ref: As soon as she touched them, her bleeding stopped, and she knew she was healed.
text: Karatetee dokoloŋ te fatabo keŋ ne gyɔ e nɛ karawɔ, te ɔ naawɔ ɔ wose man nyi ɔ kawee te taŋ.
translated: The chicken says that the hawk is not its friend, and it knows that it will not spare it when it catches it.

ref: You Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You travel over land and sea to win one follower. And when you have done so, you make that person twice as fit for hell as you are.
text: Ɛmɛɛ mmaraa wolala na Farasiitena baŋ ɛ na beo ɛ wose, ɛ laako. Nawolo nyi ɛ na mo ɛpo dɔɔ, kila tɛɛle dɔɔ pou nyi ɛ ke nyiŋ walaŋ dokoloŋ na ɔ kaa sila Wurubuarɛ. Mɔna akpaa ɛ nyiŋ walaŋ ŋonaŋ na, ɛ ne yeli ɔ dinɔɔ na bese wɔlɛɛ koraŋ keŋ nyi diyem keŋ waa naa boalaŋ man nɛ, kela ɛmɛɛ gbagba wui betabola.
translated: These are the laws that the Pharisees made and said, this is how it is. A person who has something in his eye, or a speck of dust in his eye, must go to the dokoloŋ and swear by Wurubuarɛ. If he refuses to swear there, they say he is guilty and must pay a fine of one cow and one sheep to the chief, and then the matter is settled.

ref: who will kill him. But three days later he will rise to life.” All of this made the disciples very sad. Paying the Temple Tax
text: na baa ko maŋ, mɔna weeya atoorote keŋ nɛ, Wurubuarɛ waa gyuusu maŋ lee yeŋ man.” Botɔɔ te dekɔkɔrɔkɔɔ kyaŋa ɔ kaseela baŋ.
translated: If I had known that this journey would bring such hardship, I would never have followed Wurubaare. Now I walk alone with only my thoughts.

ref: When the young man heard this, he went away sad, because he was very rich.
text: Tobaale ŋon ke nyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ, ɔ moo waraŋase lee botɔɔ nawolo nyi ɔ dana kɔba na nsendee ya.
translated: The man who has no wife is like a tree without roots, standing alone and unable to bear fruit.

ref: The crowds were surprised to hear what Jesus was teaching. The Most Important Commandment (Mark
text: Balaŋ dikpii keŋ ke nyii nombia kɛŋa nɛ, ɔ kawola keŋ doo be nɔɔ. Mmaraa dinaate Maake
translated: The child who is born today, he will grow up to be a man. The law does not allow Maake

ref: When Herod found out that the wise men from the east had tricked him, he was very angry. He gave orders for his men to kill all the boys who lived in or near Bethlehem and were two years old and younger. This was based on what he had learned from the wise men.
text: Gyoo Herode kena nyi Siakaralatena baŋ ta mo adao di ɔ dɔɔ, keŋ be ta bese kɔŋ kaa mo ɔ gyaŋ bela ya nɛ ɔ gyeŋa baŋ nideli. Keŋte ɔ kpila ɔ yoonɔɔtena nyi baa gyu Bɛtelɛhɛm donɔɔ man, na ke donɔɔsebia ŋan benaa na ke nɛ, na baa koe bia baala baŋ pou be ta bee ta kela kulutooneŋ ala ya nɛ, nawolo nyi mena debaŋ kenaŋ man te Siakaralatena baŋ yako e nyi be naa kyolobii ŋon.
translated: When Herod realized that the Magi had tricked him, he was furious, and he gave orders to kill all the boys in Bethlehem and its vicinity who were two years old and under, according to the time he had learned from the Magi. Then what was spoken through the prophet Jeremiah was fulfilled: “A voice is heard in Ramah, weeping and great mourning, Rachel weeping for her children and refusing to be comforted, because they are no more.”

ref: As bad as you are, you still know how to give good gifts to your children. But your heavenly Father is even more ready to give good things to people who ask.
text: Akpaa ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ yɛɛ nombiakumɛɛyɛɛrawɔ koraŋ gyeŋ abɔɔ kpaakpaa ka mo fa ɛ bia na, de yaa sena te ɛ kya ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ be gyae waa mo abɔɔ kpaakpaa ŋan kela kɛŋa nɛ fa baŋ bɛɛ sola e nɛ ya.
translated: When the child grows up and begins to understand things, if he does something wrong and you beat him, and later you see that he has changed and no longer does that wrong thing again, then you know the beating has benefited him.

ref: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet Isaiah had said, “These people will listen and listen, but never understand. They will look and look, but never see.
text: Nombia akɔŋaŋ balaŋ kɛwɔ dɔɔ yela Wurubuarɛ dekpeŋkpeŋgyɔɔre Yesaya nombia ŋan ɔ be yako see nɛ kɔŋawɔ ampaŋ. Yesaya yakowɔ nyiaa, “Balaŋ kɛwɔ baa tei tei, mɔna bɔɔ gyae baa nyii nombia ŋenaŋ man ya. Te baa kɛo kɛo, mɔna bɔɔ gyae baa naa kolo ya.
translated: The Lord revealed to me how the Lord Almighty showed Isaiah how the end would come. Isaiah said, “The end is coming, coming surely, and it will not delay. Though it linger, wait for it; it will certainly come and will not delay.

ref: The people of all nations will be brought before him, and he will separate them, as shepherds separate their sheep from their goats.
text: Tɛɛle kei balaŋ pou baa kaa yilaa me gyaŋ, na maa kpase be man akpeŋ ala ŋgba mena keŋ wonembia kɛɛlere ne kpase asoose lee namense man nɛ.
translated: The child went to the farm with his mother, and she told him not to eat the fruits because they were not yet ripe and would make his stomach hurt.

ref: Now hurry! Tell his disciples he has been raised to life and is on his way to Galilee. Go there, and you will see him. This is what I came to tell you.”
text: na ɛ ke ta bileŋbileŋ ke yako ɔ kaseela baŋ nyiaa ‘Ɔ te fom lee woya man te ɔ te kyaŋ ŋon gbɛɛ ɔ ne gyu Galelia tɛɛle man. Botɔɔ te ɛ kena e. Kei yɛna nombii keŋ me dana maa yako ŋon.’ ”
translated: He said to them, “This is what I told you while I was still with you: Everything must be fulfilled that is written about me in the Law of Moses, the Prophets and the Psalms.”

ref: On the way they met a man named Simon who was from Cyrene, and they forced him to carry Jesus' cross.
text: Be ka lee debɔɔ man bee gyu nɛ, te be gyaŋee na balee ŋolo ŋon ɔ ba lee Kirene te bɛɛ baake e nyi Simɔn nɛ. Keŋte ba ŋere e nyi waa lɛɛ Yesu ɔpaŋdaŋ keŋ seele.
translated: They took him to a place called Golgotha, which means the place of a skull, and they offered him wine mixed with myrrh, but he did not take it. Then they crucified him and divided his clothes among them.

ref: Although it is the smallest of all seeds, it grows larger than any garden plant and becomes a tree. Birds even come and nest on its branches.
text: Daŋbii kei yɛna bii peperee daasebia pou man. Mɔna nyi akpaa ke da bee na, kɛɛ bee bese daŋ dinaa keŋ nyi gyebuse ne kɔŋ kaa loo ba asaase do ke tombia man.”
translated: The child who is born today is the elder of tomorrow. If you do not take care of him well, one day he will grow up and become your master on this land.

ref: “Here is my chosen servant! I love him, and he pleases me. I will give him my Spirit, and he will bring justice to the nations.
text: “Kei yɛna me tobaale ŋon mɔɔ lese e, ŋon te mɛɛ gyae te me sia na gye ɔ dɔɔ. Maa mo me feliŋ do ɔ man, na waa yako debiise pou nyi debaŋ keŋ maa lɛɛ ba nyee nɛ te lii.
translated: If you see my child who has gone astray, tell them to stop what they are doing and come home. I miss them so much, and every day I cry tears that fill a big calabash, waiting for their return.

ref: Instead, store up your treasures in heaven, where moths and rust cannot destroy them, and thieves cannot break in and steal them.
text: Mɔna ɛ gyae abɔɔ see ɛ wose Wurubuarɛ man, botɔɔ keŋ paapaase na kandorɔse be wɔlɛɛ ŋa ya, te ŋmɛɛlatena mɔ be gyae baa tale gyu ke ŋmɛɛle ŋa ya nɛ.
translated: The child stopped crying when he saw Wurubuarɛ coming, but the elders said that if he had not come, they would have taken the child and thrown him into the river.

ref: So they ran all over that part of the country to bring their sick people to him on mats. They brought them each time they heard where he was.
text: Mena dɔɔ balaŋ yekiiwɔ lee tɛɛle kenaŋ yenaŋ kamasɛ man kɔŋ ɔ gyaŋ. Yenaŋ kamasɛ keŋ be nyii nyi Yesu kyaa na, bɛɛ kɔŋ na ba seele be kaweesetena ba afoŋoŋse dɔɔ bɛɛ kɔŋ botɔɔ.
translated: The people of this world are like those who have received a great treasure, but they do not know its value. They do not realize that Jesus came so that they might have life and have it more abundantly.

ref: Look at the birds in the sky! They don't plant or harvest. They don't even store grain in barns. Yet your Father in heaven takes care of them. Aren't you worth much more than birds?
text: Ɛ kɛɛ gyebuse, bɔɔ ne duu abɔɔ ya te bɔɔ ne kɔɔ ya te bɔɔ dana kpeese yaa denɛɛ ŋan man baa see weenɛɛ ya. Mɔna ɛ kya ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ ne fa wɔ weenɛɛ. Kaboena ya gyeŋ nyi, ɛ wose ne hia kela gyebuse.
translated: It is not that he is afraid, but rather that he has seen what happened to those who went before and those who came after, and so he has chosen to remain silent. But he knows that one day he too will have to speak. That is why he is not happy, for he knows his time to be afraid will come.

ref: Such large crowds gathered around him that he had to sit in a boat, while the people stood on the shore.
text: Keŋte balaŋ burum kaa yilaawɔ kilisi e. Mena dɔɔ ɔ gyoowɔ ke kyaa degbele man, na balaŋ baŋ mɔ pou seŋɛɛ dekeŋkeŋ dɔɔ.
translated: The child went to the farm and found a ripe mango. He picked it up and brought it home, and the whole family shared it equally.

ref: Homeowners never know when a thief is coming, and they are always on guard to keep one from breaking in.
text: Ɛ tɔɔse nyiaa, nyi dekpaŋalaŋte deŋ gyeŋ debaŋ keŋ ŋmɛɛlate waa kɔŋ kaa ŋmɛɛle e nɛ, nafɔ waa deke ɔ dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ nyi ŋmɛɛlate be nyiŋ gbɛɛ kaa gyoo kuu wa abɔɔ ya.
translated: All people are born free and equal in dignity and rights, and they are endowed with reason and conscience and should act towards one another in a spirit of brotherhood.

ref: He took her by the hand, and the fever left her. Then she got up and served Jesus a meal.
text: Keŋte Yesu kyaŋa e ɔ nyiŋmaa man te kpaŋalaŋ keŋ taŋawɔ. Keŋte ɔ korowɔ kɛɛ be dɔɔ.
translated: Jesus went to a village where a woman welcomed him. He was tired and hungry.

ref: Then in the middle of the night someone shouted, “Here's the groom! Come to meet him!”
text: Ke lii tɛɛ nsana nɛ te akelelee yalawɔ nyiaa, ‘Alowɔle baale ŋon te kɔŋ! Ɛ lee kaa gyaŋee na e!’
translated: If someone says to you, ‘The one who eats alone is a fool! He will surely die!’

ref: And anyone who gives one of my most humble followers a cup of cool water, just because that person is my follower, will be rewarded.
text: Walaŋ kamasɛ ŋon ɔ fa balaŋ kɛwɔ man ŋolo loŋ defɛɛsa leenɔɔ tɔɔ nyi keŋ ɔ yɛɛ me kaseele e dɔɔ nɛ, Wurubuarɛ waa tɔ e kom.”
translated: No matter how much a person may try to hide their wrongdoing, God will surely bring it to light.

ref: Jesus replied, “They don't have to leave. Why don't you give them something to eat?”
text: Keŋte Yesu yako wɔ nyiaa, “Tekaboena nyi baa ta ya. Ɛmɛɛ gbagba ɛ gyae kolo fa wɔ na baa di.”
translated: Then Jesus said to them, “A prophet is not without honor except in his own town, among his relatives and in his own home.”

ref: On the day of judgment the people of Nineveh will stand there with you and condemn you. They turned to God when Jonah preached, and yet here is something far greater than Jonah.
text: Wee keŋ Wurubuarɛ waa di balaŋ nombia nɛ, Niniwetena baa koro seŋ na baa bu gyɛŋ wee kei balaŋ fɔɔ, nawolo nyi debaŋ keŋ Gyona be kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia ŋan fa wɔ nɛ, be kyɛɛkeewɔ lee be gbɛɛneŋ kumɛɛ man te be kɔŋawɔ Wurubuarɛ gyaŋ. Mɛɛ yako ŋon baa nyiaa, walaŋ ŋon ɔ kela Gyona nɛ kyaa kɛbo.
translated: When Wurubuarɛ went to the farm one day, Niniwetena said that if they did not accompany him to the farm, he would tell Gyona to burn Wurubuarɛ’s farm so that the fire would destroy all the crops and leave Wurubuarɛ with nothing. But when they arrived, it was Gyona himself who set the fire.

ref: “Wonderful!” his master replied. “You are a good and faithful servant. I left you in charge of only a little, but now I will put you in charge of much more. Come and share in my happiness!”
text: Keŋte ɔ gbeŋgyoo ŋon yako e nyiaa, ‘Ao baale kpaakpaa anokoarete, karaŋ na keyɛɛ. N te di anokoare lee kyomii maa moo fa neŋ kei man, mena dɔɔ maa mo abɔɔ burum do n nyiŋmaa man. Kɔŋ na dee di gyoŋ keŋ mee dii nɛ.’
translated: The old man said to the child, ‘Don’t go near that river, it is very deep. I once went there with my friends and we almost drowned in the water. That place is not good for anyone to go.’

ref: The chief priests and leaders answered, “He will kill them in some horrible way. Then he will rent out his vineyard to people who will give him his share of grapes at harvest time.”
text: Keŋte be tiranɔɔ fa e nyiaa, “Waa koe mena balaŋ kumɛɛ benaŋ pou, na waa mo wɔɔ keŋ do paasetena wɔlɛɛ baŋ debaŋ de lii na, baa fa e ɔ wui nɔɔ nɛ nyiŋmaa man.”
translated: When the time came, he said, “You will all fall away because of me this night, for it is written: ‘I will strike the shepherd, and the sheep of the flock will be scattered.’”

ref: The chief priests and the whole council wanted to put Jesus to death. So they tried to find some people who would tell lies about him in court.
text: Saese kegyiise na donɔɔ kegyiise baŋ pou bɔɔ gyaŋee botɔɔ nɛ, ne gyae nombii keŋ baa mo gyakaa Yesu dɔɔ na baa nyiŋ gbɛɛ ko e.
translated: The people who were with him and the other people who were standing there saw the smoke rising up, and they took the body of Jesus and laid it in a new tomb.

ref: He was in the stomach of a big fish for three days and nights, just as the Son of Man will be deep in the earth for three days and nights.
text: Mena keŋ Gyona be doo kpebii dinaa ŋon tɔɔman weeya atooro nelim na ka weese nɛ, mena te maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon mɔ maa doo tɛɛle tɔɔman weeya atooro nelim na ka weese.
translated: When Gyona's child reached the age to go to the farm to look for food, I also took Deniwalaŋ Bu's child to go to the farm to look for food.

ref: No one knows the day or hour. The angels in heaven don't know, and the Son himself doesn't know. Only the Father knows.
text: Keŋte Yesu besewɔ yako wɔ nyiaa, ŋolo ba gyeŋ wee na debaŋ keŋ kɛŋa pou gyae ke kɔŋ ya. Wurubuarɛ kpilala baŋ be kyaa adido, na maŋ Deniwalaŋ Bu ŋon koraŋ ba gyeŋ ya. Gyisɛ me kya ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ waageŋ gyeŋa la.
translated: When Jesus saw the crowd, he told his disciples to give them something to eat, but they said they had only five loaves and two fish. Jesus took the loaves, gave thanks, and broke them, then gave them to the disciples to share. The people ate and were satisfied.

ref: But this is just the beginning of troubles.
text: Kɛŋa pou yɛɛ yaa ŋgba fulute kadoŋee kewalaŋ nɛ.
translated: The child went to the farm to harvest groundnuts.

ref: You blind Pharisee! First clean the inside of a cup, and then the outside will also be clean.
text: Ɛmɛɛ Farasiitena siayɛlɛɛsatena, ɛ taŋgbɛɛ faafo ɛ leenɔɔse na ɛ nyefalɛɛ ŋan tɔɔman na, ŋe wɔle mɔ gyae ka lee.
translated: The Farasiitena people are gathering, they are going to the river to fetch water, and they will return to the village when the sun sets.

ref: The guards shook from fear and fell down, as though they were dead.
text: Balaŋ baŋ bɛɛ deke yebɔɔ keŋ kena Wurubuarɛ kpilale ŋon nɛ, gyakoloŋ gyoo wɔ nideli te be dɔɔwɔ ba seŋɛɛ botɔɔ ŋgba woya nɛ.
translated: The time has come for all the people of Wurubuarɛ to rise up together, for the elders say that if we do not unite now, we will lose everything we have.

ref: I promise you the day of judgment will be easier for the towns of Sodom and Gomorrah than for that town. Warning about Trouble (Mark
text: Ampaŋ yaa mɛɛ yako ŋon nyi wee keŋ Wurubuarɛ waa di balaŋ nombia nɛ, waa naase mena balaŋ benaŋ diyem kela Sodom na Gomoratena diyem keŋ bɔɔ naa kaalaŋ nɛ.
translated: And you know that the people of Wurubuarɛ did not listen to the words of God, and they continued to do the same things that Sodom and Gomorrah did, which were very bad.

ref: Don't call anyone on earth your father. All of you have the same Father in heaven.
text: Te ɛ na baake ŋolo mɔ tɛɛle kei dɔɔ nyi ɛ ‘Kya’ ya, nawolo nyi ɛ dana kya dokoloŋ kooŋ bo. Ŋon yɛna ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ.
translated: If a person has a big head but their body is small, they say ‘Kya’, and the body follows the head. That is how things are done.

ref: Then so many of them came to the house that there wasn't even standing room left in front of the door. Jesus was still teaching
text: Keŋte balaŋ burum kaa wuuwɔ botɔɔ, keŋ nyi nae daseŋae be doo ya. Mena dɔɔ gbɛɛ be doo keŋ ŋolo waa mo gyoo dekpaŋalaŋ keŋ man gba ya. Te Yesu kolosi Wurubuarɛ nombia fa wɔ.
translated: The people were very surprised and said, “What kind of man is this, that even the winds and the sea obey him?” Then the disciples came and worshiped him, saying, “Truly you are the Son of God.”

ref: Jesus felt sorry for them and touched their eyes. At once they could see, and they became his followers.
text: Botɔɔ te Yesu wii be waraŋase, te ɔ moo ɔ nyiŋmaa tinaa be sia. Debokenaŋ man te be sia gyuusuwɔ, te be sila Yesu laŋ.
translated: When Jesus said to follow him, the man asked to go and bury his father first. But Jesus told him to let the dead bury their dead, and to come follow him.

ref: After Jesus had forced the demon out, the man started talking. The crowds were so amazed they began saying, “Nothing like this has ever happened in Israel!”
text: Yesu ke gegi feliŋkum keŋ lee balee ŋon man nɛ, te ɔ kolosiwɔ. Balaŋ dikpii keŋ kena mena nɛ, nombia ŋan doo be nɔɔ te be yakowɔ nyiaa, “Da te naa ta mena nombia kɛŋa see Iserae tɛɛle kei dɔɔ ya.”
translated: Jesus said to them, “Truly I tell you, at the renewal of all things, when the Son of Man sits on his glorious throne, you who have followed me will also sit on twelve thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.”

ref: At once the man's leprosy disappeared, and he was well.
text: Debokenaŋ man te ɔ kawee keŋ taŋawɔ, te ɔ nyiŋa ɔ wose.
translated: The man who owns the house is the one who has the key to the door.

ref: The next time Jesus taught beside Lake Galilee, a big crowd gathered. It was so large that he had to sit in a boat out on the lake, while the people stood on the shore.
text: Wee daale Yesu besewɔ ɔ na wola Wurubuarɛ nombia Galelia boo nɔɔman. Te mena keŋ balaŋ burum be kaa wuu ɔ dɔɔ dɔɔ nɛ, ɔ gyoowɔ ke kyaa degbele man gyu loŋ keŋ dɔɔ na balaŋ akaŋ baŋ pou seŋɛɛ dekeŋkeŋ dɔɔ.
translated: Jesus was born in Bethlehem of Judea when Herod was king. At that time some wise men came from the east to Jerusalem, asking, “Where is the child who has been born king of the Jews? We have seen his star and have come to worship him.”

ref: If a soldier forces you to carry his pack one kilometer, carry it two kilometers.
text: Akpaa ŋolo da ŋere neŋ nyi ɛmɛɛ na e ke ta nawɔɔtɔɔse kufu na, sɛɛ na ɛmɛɛ na e ke ta nawɔɔtɔɔse sao.
translated: The big dog and the goat are both going to the river to drink water, and then they will go to the farm to eat.

ref: Some Pharisees asked Jesus, “Why are your disciples picking grain on the Sabbath? They are not supposed to do that!”
text: Keŋte Farasiitena bɔɔse Yesu nyiaa, “Weera te n kaseela baŋ ne yɛɛ kpene keŋ dɛɛ kɔle nɛ da kefɛɛfowee?”
translated: Then Pilate asked Jesus, “Are you the king of the Jews?”

ref: Jesus asked, “Who is my mother and who are my brothers?”
text: Botɔɔ te Yesu bɔɔse wɔ nyiaa, “Bomɔ yɛna me naa na me naabia?”
translated: Then Jesus said to them, “Who is my mother and my brothers?”

ref: Jesus did not say a word. But the woman kept following along and shouting, so his disciples came up and asked him to send her away.
text: Mɔna Yesu te yako daale ya. Mena dɔɔ ɔ kaseela baŋ waase e nyiaa, “Lɔ alo ŋon gbɛɛ na waa ta, nawolo nyi ɔ ne fae yeesa tɔ daa deŋela.”
translated: The mother of Jesus was standing nearby. When she saw that he had died, she said to the people, “Who is the one who will not weep, for the child who has been killed for the sins of others?”

ref: When you pray, don't talk on and on as people do who don't know God. They think God likes to hear long prayers.
text: Akpaa ɛ ne fane na, ɛ na kolosi nɔɔwoya burum ŋan ŋɔɔ taŋ tɔnɔɔ, ŋgba mena keŋ balaŋ baŋ bɔɔ gyeŋ Wurubuarɛ ya ne yɛɛ nɛ ya. Baŋ gyeŋ nyi akpaa be kolosi nɔɔwoya burum na, te Wurubuarɛ waa nyii wɔ.
translated: The one who is chosen, it is he who will carry the heavy burden of this world, because he is the one who will stand before God and speak for us. Those who are not chosen to carry the heavy burden, God will not call them.

ref: The woman was Greek and had been born in the part of Syria known as Phoenicia. She begged Jesus to force the demon out of her daughter.
text: Na alo ŋonaŋ yɛɛ Giriki alo e, te ba lola e bo Fonisia keŋ ke kyaa Siria tɛɛle man nɛ. Keŋte ɔ sola Yesu nyi waa gegi feliŋkum keŋ lee ɔ bu ŋon man.
translated: It is not Greek or any other language, but the people of Phoenicia and Syria once spoke it. And they say that Jesus also used this language when he was speaking to the people.

ref: I promise that when any two of you on earth agree about something you are praying for, my Father in heaven will do it for you.
text: “Te mɛɛ yako ŋon baa bela nyiaa, kpene kamasɛ keŋ ɛ man balaŋ bala de yɛɛ nɔɔdokoloŋ seŋ ke dɔɔ fane tɛɛle kei dɔɔ na, me kya ŋon ɔ kyaa adido nɛ waa yɛɛ ke fa ŋon.”
translated: If you see that your child is going the wrong way, you should not just watch and let things continue like that until the child grows up and it becomes too late, you must take the child and show them the right way.

ref: But the chief priests and the leaders convinced the crowds to ask for Barabbas to be set free and for Jesus to be killed.
text: Mɔna Wurubuarɛ saese kegyiise na kegyiise akaŋ baŋ feefuwɔ do balaŋ dikpii keŋ nyi, baa sola Pilato na waa tina Barabase yela na baa ko Yesu.
translated: The people of Jerusalem were shouting and shouting again, asking Pilate to release Barabbas and kill Jesus.

ref: When Jesus found out what was happening, he left there and large crowds followed him. He healed all of their sick,
text: Yesu kena mena nɛ, ɔ wɔŋa ɔ wose lee yenaŋ. Balaŋ burum sila e te ɔ kyɔɔ be man kaweesetena baŋ pou,
translated: Jesus said to them, “I am the way, the truth, and the life. No one comes to the Father except through me.”

ref: Jesus did not let anyone go with him except Peter and the two brothers, James and John.
text: Mena dɔɔ Yesu te yela ŋolo ke sila e kpu na Pita na Gyemisi, na Gyemisi ɔ naabu Gyɔn baageŋ ya.
translated: Jesus took Peter, James, and his brother John up a high mountain.

ref: God blesses those people who depend only on him. They belong to the kingdom of heaven!
text: “Ɛmɛɛ baŋ ɛ ba mo ɛ wose pou lɔ Wurubuarɛ dɔɔ nɛ ɛmɛɛ na nyeebam, nawolo nyi ɛmɛɛ te Wurubuarɛ gyoori keŋ yɛɛ ɛ wui.
translated: It is because of this that if you say that God created everything and this is what you believe, then you should know that God alone is the one who kills.

ref: Jesus turned to Peter and said, “Satan, get away from me! You're in my way because you think like everyone else and not like God.”
text: Botɔɔ te Yesu gɛɛsewɔ kɛɛ Pita te ɔ yako e nyiaa, “Ɔbɔnsam, ta lee me gyaŋ! N ne gyae n ke tɔ maŋ gbɛɛ. Nawolo nyi na Wurubuarɛ kegyaebii te n na gyueŋ ya, mɔna denibalaŋ kegyaebii te n na gyueŋ.”
translated: But Jesus turned and said to Peter, “Get behind me, Satan! You are a stumbling block to me; you do not have in mind the concerns of God, but merely human concerns.”
